title: Agent Hooks
subtitle: Lifecycle hooks: block, observe, inject context across agent events

beta.title: Hooks (Beta)
beta.description: Hooks let you intercept agent lifecycle events to allow, block, or rewrite what happens. Currently in beta — expect rough edges and watch your audit log.
beta.howItWorksBody1: Each hook fires on one event such as user_prompt_submit, pre_tool_use, or stop. Blocking events wait for your decision before continuing.
beta.howItWorksBody2: Script runs sandboxed JavaScript. HTTP posts to your webhook. Prompt asks an LLM. Built-in hooks (System badge) ship with GoClaw and only the enabled toggle is editable.
beta.howItWorksBody3: Use Run hook with a sample event to preview the decision, the duration, and any input mutations — nothing touches a real session until enabled.
beta.learnMore: How hooks work with Skills & MCP
beta.modalIntro: Hooks integrate with GoClaw's Skill system and MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to give you fine-grained control over the agent pipeline.
beta.modalSkillBody: When a skill is invoked, the agent fires pre_tool_use and post_tool_use events. A hook on pre_tool_use can inspect the skill name and arguments, then allow, block, or rewrite them before execution. Post-tool hooks observe results for logging or chaining.
beta.modalMcpBody: MCP tools registered from external servers appear as regular tools in the agent pipeline. Hooks fire on the same pre_tool_use / post_tool_use events — no special wiring needed. Use a matcher regex to target specific MCP tool names (e.g. mcp__.*).
beta.modalBuiltinBody: Built-in hooks like the PII Redactor run as sandboxed scripts with elevated privileges: they can rewrite user input (rawInput) and tool arguments before the agent sees them. User-created scripts cannot — this is enforced at the dispatcher level.

form.name: Name
form.agentsHint: Select which agents this hook applies to
form.matcherHint: Applied to tool name before firing (optional except for prompt)
form.scriptSchemaHelp: Export function handle(event) returning {decision: "allow"|"block", reason?: string, updatedInput?: object}. Limit 32 KiB.
form.builtinReadonly: Built-in hook — only the enabled toggle can be changed.

test.title: Test hook
test.sampleEventHint: Craft a fake event to fire the hook against — nothing here touches a real session.
test.toolNameHint: Used by pre/post_tool_use matchers (e.g. bash, edit, web_search).
test.toolInputHint: Arguments passed to the tool. Must be valid JSON.
test.rawInputHint: Used by user_prompt_submit hooks. Leave blank for tool events.
test.parseError: Tool input must be valid JSON
test.resultHint: What the dispatcher returned, including any input mutations from builtin hooks.
test.emptyState: Run the hook to see how it would respond.
test.overwriteConfirm: Current input will be replaced with template. Continue?

validation.promptRequiresMatcher: Prompt hooks require a matcher or if_expr
